name: LS prod - 2nd message trigger
description: Template version of the CS 2nd-message trigger workflow. Reads project-specific config from the cs_project_config data table via project_key. Handles follow-up customer messages on Zendesk tickets, gated by a configurable message count threshold.

values.content: =Description: {{ $("Check ticket language1").item.json["body"] }}

You are a translation engine.

Your sole task is to translate user-provided text into English.

Rules (must be followed strictly):

Translate exactly and literally

Do not add, remove, infer, or reinterpret any information

Do not explain, summarize, rephrase, or improve wording

Preserve tone, intent, ambiguity, and emotional content exactly

Preserve formatting, punctuation, emojis, and line breaks

Do not normalize slang, profanity, or errors

If a term cannot be translated precisely, transliterate it

If the input is already in English, return it unchanged

If the input is partially English, translate only the non-English parts

Output needs to contain:
description: string // translated description,
originalTicketLanguage: string // two letter code for the language of the original ticket

Needs to be formatted as JSON

No commentary, labels, or notes

No markdown unless it exists in the input

You are a customer support ticket classifier.

Classify the customer message into exactly one category:

1. "thanks"
Use this only when the customer is simply expressing gratitude and is not asking for anything else.
Examples:
- “Thanks for your help.”
- “Many thanks for your prompt response.”
- “Thank you for processing the refund. I appreciate it.”

2. "confirmation"
Use this when the customer is asking support to verify, confirm, or check the status of something already expected or completed, such as a cancellation, refund, transaction reversal, account closure, subscription status, or payment status.
Examples:
- “I’m reaching out to confirm my subscription has been canceled.”
- “Can you confirm my refund was processed?”
- “Just checking that my transaction has been refunded.”
- “Please confirm my account is closed.”

3. "complaint"
Use this when the customer expresses dissatisfaction, frustration, anger, disappointment, distrust, or negative sentiment about the service, support, billing, refund, cancellation, charges, delays, product, company, or experience.
Examples:
- “This is unacceptable.”
- “I was charged again after canceling.”
- “Your support has been terrible.”
- “I’m frustrated that my refund still hasn’t arrived.”

4. "other"
Use this as the fallback when the ticket does not clearly fit thanks, confirmation, or complaint.
Examples:
- New cancellation request
- New refund request
- Account update request
- Product question
- Feature question
- Technical question
- General inquiry
- Unclear or incomplete message

Decision rules:
- Use only the customer’s title and description.
- Choose "thanks" only if the message contains gratitude and no request, complaint, question, escalation, or unresolved issue.
- If the message contains both gratitude and a confirmation/check request, choose "confirmation".
- If the message contains any meaningful negative sentiment or complaint, choose "complaint", even if it also includes thanks or asks for confirmation.
- If the customer asks support to verify whether something happened, choose "confirmation".
- If the customer is making a new request rather than confirming an existing action, choose "other".
- If the message is unclear, incomplete, or does not fit the first three categories, choose "other".

Output requirements:
Return only a valid JSON object with exactly one key:
{
  "category": "thanks" | "confirmation" | "complaint" | "other"
}

Do not include explanations, evidence, markdown, or extra keys.

=Customer message: {{ $json.data.first().translated_data.is_translated?$('Aggregate').item.json.data.first().translated_data.translations.description:$('Aggregate').item.json.data.first().original_ticket_content.description }}

You are an information extraction system for customer support messages.

Analyze the customer support message and extract the customer's email address if one is explicitly provided.

Return only a JSON object that matches the provided schema.

Rules:
- The output must contain exactly one key: customer_email.
- If the customer message contains a valid email address, set customer_email to that email address as a string.
- If the customer message does not contain a valid email address, set customer_email to false.
- Do not include markdown, explanations, comments, or extra text.
- Do not guess, infer, reconstruct, or fabricate an email address.
- If multiple valid email addresses are present return false
=Customer message: {{ $('Return last comment').item.json.body }}

values.content:
You are a customer support ticket classifier. You analyze customer support messages and identify if they are refund requests or not based only on the user’s written message

Refunds (r)
Use this category if the customer is asking for money back, a refund, a charge reversal, or mentions being charged and wanting the charge undone.  

Other  (o)
Use this category for all other messages like:
account deletion requests
product questions
feature requests
account access issues
complaints 
unclear or ambiguous messages
harassment or threatening

IMPORTANT RULES:
If a refund is requested or clearly implied, choose Refunds category
Do NOT assume a refund request from frustration or complaints alone.
Do NOT assume a refund from a cancellation request unless money back is explicitly requested.
If the intent is unclear or ambiguous, choose Other 
If the message contains forwarded content (often labelled with "Forwarded message") disregard everything that comes after that and only consider the content before it in the classification

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Output only one letter based on the category of the ticket, the output must be a single token
r = Refunds
o = Other

Return only a valid JSON object with exactly one key:
{
  "request_category": "o" | "r" 
}

Do not include explanations, evidence, markdown, or extra keys. Output only the letter.

=Customer message: {{$('Aggregate').item.json.data[0].translated_data.is_translated?$('Aggregate').item.json.data.first().translated_data.translations.description:$('Aggregate').item.json.data.first().original_ticket_content.description  }}

- Thanks
- Cancel/refund confirmation
- Complaint
- Acc/pass info

If the ticket DOES fit into any of the category above please reach out to the Operations Dept. 

Please handle this ticket manually.

parameters.content:
# Template configuration

## How this template works
1. **Set project_key** holds the hardcoded key for this deployment.
2. **Get Project Config** fetches the row from the `cs_project_config` data table.
3. All downstream nodes reference values via `$('Get Project Config').first().json.column_name`.

## To duplicate for a new project
- Duplicate this workflow.
- Change the value in **Set project_key**.
- Add/update a row in `cs_project_config` for that key.
- Update the Zendesk Trigger credentials and webhook ID.
- Note: `ticket.ticket_field_*` IDs in the Zendesk Trigger fields list are hardcoded (trigger params do not allow expressions). Update them for the new project.

## Convention values kept hardcoded
- `refund_auto`, `cancellation_auto`, `other_auto` automation_classification values are produced by the main automation flow and consumed here as cross-workflow constants.

## Status: solved
## Macro: single refund complaint
# Requested confirmation but subs is active

## Status: open
## Category: none
## Assign to CS

## Status: open
## Category: none
## Assign to CS

## Status: open
## Category: none
## Assign to CS

## Status: open
## Category: none
## Assign to CS

## Status: open
## Category: none
## Assign to CS

## Status: solved
## Macro: double refund complaint

You are a customer support ticket classifier. You analyze customer support messages and identify if they are cancellation requests or not based only on the user’s written message

Cancellations (c)
Use this category if the customer is asking for subscription cancellation, account cancellation, if they want to cancel, mentions being charged for something they didn't want or mentions stopping payments

Other  (o)
Use this category for all other messages like:
account deletion requests
product questions
feature requests
account access issues
complaints 
unclear or ambiguous messages
harassment or threatening

IMPORTANT RULES:
If a cancellation is requested or clearly implied, choose Cancellation category
Do NOT assume a cancellation request from frustration or complaints alone.
If the intent is unclear or ambiguous, choose Other 
If the message contains forwarded content (often labelled with "Forwarded message") disregard everything that comes after that and only consider the content before it in the classification

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Output only one letter based on the category of the ticket, the output must be a single token
c = cancellation
o = Other

Return only a valid JSON object with exactly one key:
{
  "request_category": "o" | "c" 
}

Do not include explanations, evidence, markdown, or extra keys. Output only the letter.

=Customer message: {{$('Aggregate').item.json.data[0].translated_data.is_translated?$('Aggregate').item.json.data.first().translated_data.translations.description:$('Aggregate').item.json.data.first().original_ticket_content.description  }}

body.ticket.title: Fw: Your Lovestyle.co subscription has been canceled
body.ticket.description:
----------------------------------------------

Klapoint1112, May 18, 2026, 17:32

Hi, I am still be charged for this.



Can you please refund the payments that have been charged after January?







----- Forwarded Message -----

**From:** Kathryn LaPoint <klapoint1112@yahoo.com>

**To:** Michael Stein <mstein0713@gmail.com>

**Sent:** Sunday, March 1, 2026 at 10:38:48 AM CST

**Subject:** Fw: Your Lovestyle.co subscription has been canceled



Did you look into this?  remember they charged me?  Do you have any emails from this company?









----- Forwarded Message -----

**From:** LoveStyle <noreply@lovestyle.co>

**To:**"klapoint1112@yahoo.com" <klapoint1112@yahoo.com>

**Sent:** Tuesday, January 27, 2026 at 08:04:14 AM CST

**Subject:** Your Lovestyle.co subscription has been canceled



 ![Lovestyle.co logo](https://lovestyle.co/api/static/email-logo-v1.png)

## Dear MS,

We're writing to confirm that your subscription with Lovestyle.co has **been successfully canceled**. You will not be charged any further for your subscription.

We're sorry to see you go, but we want to ensure you that you can still access our free resources and tools that support your journey of romantic discovery until the end of your current billing period. Your subscription will remain active until February 22, 2026, after which your access to premium features, tests, and reports will be discontinued.

If you ever decide to return, know that Lovestyle.co will be here to welcome you back with open arms.

Should you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to reach out to our support team at support@lovestyle.co.

Thank you for being a part of our community.

Wishing you all the best,
 The Lovestyle.co Team

 ![](http://url5068.lovestyle.co/wf/open?upn=u001.jUi6xgd8XthkwNHJSPtvDwcRiWG1m1cN001YIbsdw6lisTCe0UQXI6nrJ8lL-2BRpLJPWepik-2BSpj3A1cthUWS0J0IhixHMT58bArsMJ5-2Fvkr4QxBxT723tCPlN56fmubjkYXYejgTE-2FD3-2B4C-2FHERJLO4kmUhXDCf-2Bh-2BCtT2jBTZE4E9C6aDe5pU8h9Y3jA0S2GQjm0wqUtIdS6gPWjlOIurn-2F-2FCz3TOPw4EAH7ZR6nNM-3D)
